believe it can be done.explanation_en: to have confidence that something is possible to achieve
believe it can be done.example_sentence: If you believe it can be done, you are halfway to success.
believe it can be done.original_sentence: Step one, believe it can be done.

basic truth.explanation_en: a fundamental fact or principle that is always true
basic truth.example_sentence: Honesty is a basic truth that everyone should follow.

sets the mind in motion.explanation_en: causes someone to start thinking about something or to begin planning
sets the mind in motion.explanation_zh: 促使头脑开始思考/行动
sets the mind in motion.example_sentence: The new challenge sets his mind in motion to find solutions.
sets the mind in motion.original_sentence: Believing something can be done sets the mind in motion to find a way to do it.

illustrate this point.explanation_en: to use an example or explanation to make a particular idea or argument clearer
illustrate this point.example_sentence: The teacher used a story to illustrate this point about courage.
illustrate this point.original_sentence: To illustrate this point of creative thinking in training sessions, I often use this example.

creative thinking.explanation_en: the ability to think of new and original ideas
creative thinking.example_sentence: Creative thinking is essential for solving complex problems.
creative thinking.original_sentence: To illustrate this point of creative thinking in training sessions, I often use this example.

training sessions.explanation_en: meetings or periods of time where people are taught skills or knowledge
training sessions.example_sentence: We have weekly training sessions to improve our teamwork.
training sessions.original_sentence: To illustrate this point of creative thinking in training sessions, I often use this example.

feel it is possible.explanation_en: to believe that something can happen or be achieved
feel it is possible.example_sentence: Do you feel it is possible to finish the project on time?
feel it is possible.original_sentence: I ask the group, how many of you feel it is possible to eliminate jails within the next 30 years?

eliminate.explanation_en: to completely remove or get rid of something
eliminate.example_sentence: We need to eliminate waste from our daily lives.
eliminate.original_sentence: I ask the group, how many of you feel it is possible to eliminate jails within the next 30 years?

invariably.example_sentence: He invariably arrives on time for meetings.
invariably.original_sentence: Invariably, the group looks bewildered, not quite sure they heard right and thinking they are listening to a real fuzzbrain.

bewildered.example_sentence: She looked bewildered when she saw the unexpected result.
bewildered.original_sentence: Invariably, the group looks bewildered, not quite sure they heard right and thinking they are listening to a real fuzzbrain.

not quite sure.example_sentence: He was not quite sure which road to take.
not quite sure.original_sentence: Invariably, the group looks bewildered, not quite sure they heard right and thinking they are listening to a real fuzzbrain.

fuzzbrain.explanation_en: a silly or foolish person (informal, slightly derogatory)
fuzzbrain.explanation_zh: 糊涂蛋，傻瓜（非正式，略带贬义）
fuzzbrain.example_sentence: He thought his idea was brilliant, but others considered him a bit of a fuzzbrain.
fuzzbrain.original_sentence: Invariably, the group looks bewildered, not quite sure they heard right and thinking they are listening to a real fuzzbrain.

after a pause.explanation_en: after a short period of silence or stopping
after a pause.example_sentence: After a pause, she continued her speech.
after a pause.original_sentence: So, after a pause, I repeat, how many of you feel it is possible to eliminate jails within the next thirty years?

joking.explanation_en: saying something for amusement, not seriously
joking.example_sentence: Don't worry, I'm just joking with you.
joking.original_sentence: Once they're sure I'm not joking, someone always blasts me with something like,

blasts me with.explanation_en: to strongly criticize or attack someone with words
blasts me with.example_sentence: The audience blasted the politician with tough questions.
blasts me with.original_sentence: Once they're sure I'm not joking, someone always blasts me with something like,

you mean to say.explanation_en: used to express surprise or disbelief at what someone has said
you mean to say.example_sentence: You mean to say you're quitting your job?
you mean to say.original_sentence: you mean to say you want to turn all those murderers, thieves, and rapists loose?

turn loose.explanation_en: to release someone or something; to let them go free
turn loose.example_sentence: The zoo had to turn loose the escaped animals back into the wild.
turn loose.original_sentence: you mean to say you want to turn all those murderers, thieves, and rapists loose?

murderers.explanation_en: people who have committed murder (the crime of killing someone unlawfully)
murderers.example_sentence: The police are searching for the murderers.
murderers.original_sentence: you mean to say you want to turn all those murderers, thieves, and rapists loose?

thieves.example_sentence: The shop has security cameras to catch thieves.
thieves.original_sentence: you mean to say you want to turn all those murderers, thieves, and rapists loose?

rapists.explanation_en: people who have committed rape (the crime of forcing someone to have sexual intercourse)
rapists.original_sentence: you mean to say you want to turn all those murderers, thieves, and rapists loose?

don't you realize.explanation_en: used to express surprise or to point out something obvious that someone seems to be ignoring
don't you realize.example_sentence: Don't you realize how late it is?
don't you realize.original_sentence: Don't you realize what this would mean?

none of us.explanation_zh: 我们中的任何一个都不
none of us.example_sentence: None of us knew the answer to the difficult question.
none of us.original_sentence: Why none of us would be safe.

safe.explanation_en: protected from or not exposed to danger or risk; not likely to be harmed or lost
safe.example_sentence: It's important to feel safe in your own home.
safe.original_sentence: Why none of us would be safe.

have to have.explanation_en: to need something very much; it is necessary
have to have.example_sentence: We have to have a plan before we start the project.

cut loose.explanation_en: to behave in a wild or unrestrained way; to become free from restrictions
cut loose.example_sentence: After the exams, the students decided to cut loose and celebrate.

order.explanation_en: a state of peace and obedience to laws or rules
order.example_sentence: Without rules, society would descend into chaos and lose all order.
order.original_sentence: All order would break down if we didn't have jails.

break down.explanation_en: to stop functioning (for a system or machine); to fail
break down.example_sentence: If we don't have laws, society will break down.
break down.original_sentence: All order would break down if we didn't have jails.

born criminals.explanation_en: people who are believed to have a natural tendency to commit crimes
born criminals.example_sentence: The idea that some people are born criminals is controversial.

if anything.explanation_en: used to suggest that a statement is true, or that something is the case, to an even greater extent
if anything.explanation_zh: 如果说有什么的话，（更是如此）
if anything.example_sentence: The situation isn't bad; if anything, it's getting better.
if anything.original_sentence: If anything, we need more jails.

murder.explanation_en: the crime of killing a person deliberately and unlawfully
murder.example_sentence: The news reported a shocking murder in the city.
murder.original_sentence: Did you read in this morning's paper about that murder?

goes on.explanation_en: continues to happen or be said
goes on.example_sentence: The discussion goes on for hours.

all sorts of.example_sentence: She has all sorts of hobbies.
all sorts of.original_sentence: telling me all sorts of good reasons why we must have jails.

good reasons.example_sentence: He gave good reasons for his decision.
good reasons.original_sentence: telling me all sorts of good reasons why we must have jails.

suggested.explanation_en: put forward an idea or a plan for someone to consider
suggested.example_sentence: He suggested a new approach to the problem.
suggested.original_sentence: One fellow even suggested we've got to have jails so the police and prison guards can have jobs.

prison guards.explanation_en: people who are employed to watch prisoners and make sure they do not escape
prison guards.example_sentence: The prison guards are responsible for the safety of the inmates.
prison guards.original_sentence: One fellow even suggested we've got to have jails so the police and prison guards can have jobs.

have jobs.explanation_en: to be employed; to have work to do
have jobs.example_sentence: Many people in the town have jobs at the local factory.
have jobs.original_sentence: One fellow even suggested we've got to have jails so the police and prison guards can have jobs.

after about.explanation_en: used to indicate an approximate amount of time
after about.example_sentence: After about an hour, we arrived at our destination.
after about.original_sentence: After about ten minutes of letting the group prove why we can't eliminate the need for jails,

prove.explanation_en: to demonstrate the truth or existence of something by evidence or argument
prove.example_sentence: Can you prove that your theory is correct?
prove.original_sentence: After about ten minutes of letting the group prove why we can't eliminate the need for jails,

the need for.explanation_en: the requirement or necessity for something
the need for.example_sentence: There is a great need for more affordable housing.
the need for.original_sentence: After about ten minutes of letting the group prove why we can't eliminate the need for jails,

mention.explanation_en: to refer to something briefly and without going into detail
mention.example_sentence: He didn't mention the problem during the meeting.
mention.original_sentence: I say to them, Now, let me mention here that this question of eliminating jails is used to make a point.

make a point.explanation_en: to express your opinion or state your case clearly
make a point.example_sentence: He wanted to make a point about the importance of recycling.
make a point.original_sentence: I say to them, Now, let me mention here that this question of eliminating jails is used to make a point.

come up with.explanation_en: to think of or produce an idea, plan, or solution
come up with.example_sentence: She always comes up with creative solutions.
come up with.original_sentence: Each of you has come up with reasons why we can't eliminate the need for jails.

do me a favor.explanation_en: to do something helpful for someone
do me a favor.example_sentence: Could you do me a favor and pick up some milk?
do me a favor.original_sentence: Will you do me a favor?

try extra hard.explanation_en: to make a special effort; to work more diligently
try extra hard.example_sentence: You'll have to try extra hard to pass this exam.
try extra hard.original_sentence: Will you try extra hard for a few minutes to believe we can eliminate jails?

joining in the spirit of.explanation_en: participating in something with enthusiasm and in the same mood or attitude as others
joining in the spirit of.example_sentence: Joining in the spirit of the holiday, everyone wore festive costumes.
joining in the spirit of.original_sentence: Joining in the spirit of the experiment, the group says, in effect, all well, but just for kicks.

in effect.example_sentence: The new policy is, in effect, a tax increase.
in effect.original_sentence: Joining in the spirit of the experiment, the group says, in effect, all well, but just for kicks.

for kicks.example_sentence: We went hiking just for kicks.
for kicks.original_sentence: Joining in the spirit of the experiment, the group says, in effect, all well, but just for kicks.

assuming.explanation_en: supposing that something is true, without proof
assuming.example_sentence: Assuming you are right, what should we do next?
assuming.original_sentence: Then I ask, now, assuming we can eliminate jails, how could we begin?

how could we begin.explanation_en: what would be the first steps to start something
how could we begin.example_sentence: Assuming we have the budget, how could we begin the construction?
how could we begin.original_sentence: Then I ask, now, assuming we can eliminate jails, how could we begin?
